The cloudberry (Rubus chamaemorus) is a low-growing, Arctic shrub native to the northern hemisphere, producing small yellow-orange fruit. The health benefits of cloudberry include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and potential anti-cancer properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of cloudberry include ellagic acid, quercetin, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with cloudberry include jams, preserves, and extracts, as well as teas and tinctures used in traditional medicine.
